controls.title: MFA required for privileged users and Microsoft 365 services
controls.description: Checks whether Conditional Access policies exist that require MFA for privileged users and for access to Microsoft 365 services, as required by the Essential Eight MFA mitigation strategy. Addresses the enforcement gap between CIS MFA capability checks and the Essential Eight outcome-based requirement.
controls.notes: Checks for MFA enforcement via Conditional Access. Policies with broad user exclusions are flagged in details for assessor review. Research basis: 26T1-SEC-EG-001 and 26T1-SEC-EG-003.

controls.title: Privileged access restricted and appropriately assigned
controls.description: Checks whether administrative privileges are limited to an appropriate set of users by evaluating Entra ID directory role assignments. Assesses the distribution of privileged users across high-impact roles.
controls.notes: Research complete (26T1-SEC-EG-002, 26T1-SEC-EG-004). Rego policy and implementation pending. Threshold for compliant vs non-compliant to be defined at implementation time.

controls.title: Patch Applications — ML1
controls.description: Patches for security vulnerabilities in internet-facing services applied within two weeks of release.

controls.title: Patch Operating Systems — ML1
controls.description: Patches for internet-facing services applied within two weeks. Unsupported operating systems not used.

controls.title: Application Control — ML1
controls.description: Application control implemented on workstations to restrict execution to approved applications.

controls.title: Restrict Microsoft Office Macros — ML1
controls.description: Macros disabled for users who do not require them. Only macros from trusted locations permitted.

controls.title: User Application Hardening — ML1
controls.description: Web browsers configured to block ads and Java. Web-based technologies restricted where not required.

controls.title: Regular Backups — ML1
controls.description: Backups of important data performed and retained for at least three months.
